name: Santa Maria de los Llanos – circular via the Cerro Agudo trail (PR-CU 68)
name_original: Santa María de los Llanos – circular por el sendero del Cerro Agudo (PR-CU 68)
region_intro: Hiking around Santa María De Los Llanos offers exploration across the gentle, rolling plains of Spain's La Mancha region. The landscape is characterized by significant wetland complexes, including the protected Manjavacas Lagoon, which provide diverse aquatic environments. These natural features, combined with the area's connection to cultural heritage, define the terrain for outdoor activities. The region provides a blend of serene natural beauty and moderate trails for hikers. Best hiking trails around Santa María De Los Llanos The most popular hiking route is Santa Maria de los Llanos – circular via the Cerro Agudo trail (PR-CU 68), a 7.1 miles (11.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 58 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the local flora and fauna characteristic of the La Mancha plains directly from the town. Another top favourite among local hikers is Lagunar de Manjavacas – circular by the Camino Natural de los Humedales de La Mancha, a moderate 10.5 miles (16.9 km) path. This trail explores the protected Manjavacas Lagoon complex, offering opportunities for birdwatching and tranquil scenery. Local hikers also love the Mota del Cuervo – circular by the mills of La Sierra, a 2.5 miles (4.1 km) trail leading through the area around the iconic windmills, often completed in about 1 hour 6 minutes. Hiking around Santa María De Los Llanos is defined by rolling plains, significant wetlands, and cultural landmarks like windmills. The network offers options for easy and moderate ability levels. The routes in Santa María De Los Llanos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.4 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 20 hikers have used komoot to explore Santa María De Los Llanos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Santa María De Los Llanos?
A: There are 8 hiking trails around Santa María De Los Llanos. These routes offer a variety of experiences across the gentle plains and wetlands of the La Mancha region.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of hiking trails in Santa María De Los Llanos?
A: The hiking trails in Santa María De Los Llanos are predominantly easy to moderate. Out of 8 routes, 1 is classified as easy and 7 are moderate, making the area accessible for most hikers.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in Santa María De Los Llanos are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013172757/santa-maria-de-los-llanos-circular-via-the-cerro-agudo-trail-pr-cu-68" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Maria de los Llanos – circular via the Cerro Agudo trail (PR-CU 68)&lt;/a&gt; offers a dedicated path directly from the town, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013169581/lagunar-de-manjavacas-circular-by-the-camino-natural-de-los-humedales-de-la-mancha" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagunar de Manjavacas – circular by the Camino Natural de los Humedales de La Mancha&lt;/a&gt; explores the protected lagoon complex.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see while hiking?
A: The region is known for its significant wetland complexes, particularly the Manjavacas Lagoon Complex. You can explore the "Camino Natural de los Humedales de La Mancha" which offers routes around these diverse aquatic environments, providing excellent opportunities for birdwatching and enjoying tranquil scenery.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, beyond the natural beauty, Santa María de los Llanos is close to several cultural and historical attractions. You can visit the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5421900" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Windmills&lt;/a&gt; of Mota del Cuervo, or the majestic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4882732" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Belmonte&lt;/a&gt;, both within easy reach and offering a glimpse into the region's rich heritage.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Santa María De Los Llanos?
A: The hiking routes in Santa María De Los Llanos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.4 stars from over 7 reviews. Hikers often praise the serene natural beauty and the well-maintained paths, especially around the wetlands.

Q: Are there any easy routes suitable for a quick walk?
A: Yes, for an easier and shorter walk, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013164664/mota-del-cuervo-circular-by-the-mills-of-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mota del Cuervo – circular by the mills of La Sierra&lt;/a&gt;. This 4.1 km (2.5 miles) trail takes approximately 1 hour and 6 minutes to complete and leads through the area around the iconic windmills.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Santa María De Los Llanos?
A: The gentle, rolling plains of La Mancha are generally pleasant for hiking during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. These seasons also offer the best conditions for birdwatching in the wetland areas.

Q: Can I see wildlife on the trails?
A: The Manjavacas Lagoon Complex is a protected nature reserve crucial for biodiversity. Hikers exploring routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013169581/lagunar-de-manjavacas-circular-by-the-camino-natural-de-los-humedales-de-la-mancha" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Natural de los Humedales de La Mancha&lt;/a&gt; often encounter various bird species and other wetland wildlife.

Q: Are there any trails that start directly from Santa María de los Llanos town?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013172757/santa-maria-de-los-llanos-circular-via-the-cerro-agudo-trail-pr-cu-68" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Maria de los Llanos – circular via the Cerro Agudo trail (PR-CU 68)&lt;/a&gt; is a dedicated path that starts directly from the town, offering views of the local flora and fauna characteristic of the La Mancha plains.

Q: What is the longest moderate hike available?
A: The longest moderate hike is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013169581/lagunar-de-manjavacas-circular-by-the-camino-natural-de-los-humedales-de-la-mancha" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagunar de Manjavacas – circular by the Camino Natural de los Humedales de La Mancha&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 16.9 km (10.5 miles) and typically takes around 4 hours and 18 minutes to complete.
tips: Located in the town of Santa María de los Llanos, the Church is a clear example of 16th century Renaissance architecture, although its semicircular apse is the oldest part and dates from the Romanesque period. Inside it is very beautiful and worth visiting, its main altarpiece made of polychrome wood stands out.

waypoints.name: Church of Our Lady of the Assumption
